item.title: n8n Sandbox Escape: Critical Vulnerabilities in n8n Exposes Hundreds of Thousands of Enterprise AI Systems to Complete Takeover
item.description: Pillar Security researchers uncovered critical vulnerabilities in n8n, a popular open-source workflow automation platform powering numerous enterprise deployments. The vulnerabilities allowed any authenticated user to seize complete control of the server, stealing every stored credential, API key, and secret on both self hosted and cloud instances. On n8n Cloud, the shared multi-tenant architecture meant a single malicious user could potentially breach the entire platform, accessing data belonging to all other customers.

item.title: New 'Rules File Backdoor' Attack Lets Hackers Inject Malicious Code via AI Code Editors
item.description: New 'Rules File Backdoor' Attack Lets Hackers Inject Malicious Code via AI Code Editors

item.title: Pillar Security is Now Available on the AWS Marketplace
item.description: Pillar Security is Now Available on the AWS Marketplace

item.title: Pillar Security is Now Available in the Microsoft Azure Marketplace
item.description: We're excited to announce that Pillar Security platform is now available in the Microsoft Azure Marketplace, enabling organizations to strengthen their AI security through Azure's enterprise-grade infrastructure.‍ Pillar’s customers can now take advantage of the scalability, reliability, and security of Azure, with streamlined deployment and management. Azure customers can allocate their Microsoft Azure consumption commitment (MACC) spend to purchase Pillar’s AI security platform

item.title: Pillar Security Raises $9M to Help Companies Build and Run Secure AI Software
item.description: Pillar Security Raises $9M to Help Companies Build and Run Secure AI Software

item.title: Pillar Security Unveils RedGraph: The World’s First Attack Surface Mapping & Continuous Testing for AI Agents
item.description: Today we're excited to announce RedGraph, the world's first attack surface mapping and testing tool for AI agents that enables security teams to discover and validate real exploits in their AI agents continuously. It interacts with AI systems through the browser and works with any web-accessible AI agent, including those built on Microsoft Copilot, Salesforce Agentforce, Google Agentspace, n8n, and more. All findings are exploit-validated and flow directly into Pillar's adaptive guardrails, creating a closed loop where defenses improve continuously alongside the testing.

item.title: Pillar Security's Enhanced Amazon Bedrock Integration: Complete AI Security and Governance
item.description: Pillar Security's Enhanced Amazon Bedrock Integration: Complete AI Security and Governance

item.title: Pillar Selected for the AWS & CrowdStrike Cybersecurity Accelerator

item.title: Practical AI Red Teaming: The Power of Multi-Turn Tests vs Single-Turn Evaluations
item.description: Discover the vital role of red-teaming in securing Large Language Models (LLMs). This blog explores the differences between single-turn and multi-turn AI red-teaming, examines advanced techniques like MART and DART, highlights vulnerabilities against multi-turn human jailbreaks, and showcases Pillar Security's innovative solutions for robust AI protection. Learn how comprehensive red-teaming strategies enhance AI safety and trustworthiness in evolving technological landscapes.

item.title: LLMs Are An Essential Kernel Process Of A New Operating System
item.description: Discover how Large Language Models (LLMs) are transforming into essential components of new operating systems. Explore their multi-modal capabilities, code execution, internet access, memory storage, and crucial security concepts. Understand the paradigm shift in computing and the need for robust, human-aligned AI security measures to build the next generation of AI-powered platforms

item.title: Manipulating LLM Agents: A Case Study in Prompt Injection Attacks
item.description: Manipulating LLM Agents: A Case Study in Prompt Injection Attacks
